metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_5491
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1: "These data show that the monosynaptic EPSC amplitude was increased in neurons expressing the reduced Ca affinity mutant CaSR. Furthermore the data indicate that CaSR was not fully activated at basal [Ca] and [Mg] in CaSR-/- neurons."

Figure 2: "Thus the majority of the CaSR genotype-dependent difference in EPSC amplitude may be attributed to an increase in release probability in CaSR-/- cultures."

Figure 3, 4: CaSR activation attenuates synaptic transmission.

Figure 5: CaSR activation enhances paired-pulse depression.

Figure 7: authors propose a model where extracellular calcium sensing receptor (CaSR), a G-protein-coupled receptor, blocks the activation of voltage-dependent nonselective cation channels (NSCC).

"In summary, reduced CaSR function enhanced synaptic transmission and CaSR stimulation had the opposite effect. Thus CaSR provides a mechanism that may compensate for the fall in release probability that accompanies decreases in extracellular calcium.". Experimental description: M&M: "Neocortical cell cultures were prepared (Mermelstein et al., 2000) from postnatal day 1 (P1) to P2 wild-type or CaSR-/- mice"
